Instructions & Glossary.Clover Inventory Import/Export: Instructions: Complete each tab of the sheet with inventory information. Once complete, upload your file to your dashboard by following the instructions here. Note: All items may be edited after upload in your dashboard.

Instructions & Glossary.__EMPTY: Description

An alternative item name can be used for internal shorthand, or for employees who speak a different language. To display the Alternative Item Name, on your POS device go to: Setup app > Order Receipts and select Use alternative inventory names.
A group of similar items.
Set category names to group similar items in your inventory or menu. Examples of categories are 'breakfast', 'lunch', 'adult', or 'child'.
Leave blank.
Would you like to apply a default tax rate? If so, mark as "Yes."
A brief, customer-friendly description of your item.
This is used to indicate if an item is shown on the POS.
The name of an item.
Used for variants; the item you would like to add variants/variations for. Every item group must have a unique name.
A list of items within a category, sorted in the order you would like them to appear on your POS device.

Modifiers are adjustments that can be made to an item; modifiers are often required to customize a restaurant order. An example of a modifier is the temperature of a steak (rare/medium/well-done), the option of cheese on a hamburger, or a choice of salad dressing.
Non-revenue items are items that are "sold" but don't generate revenue. Some examples of non-revenue items are take-out containers or to-go cups.
Enter "yes" on modifiers that are required to be selected for an order. For example, the prepared temperature of a steak might be a required modifier.
The price you charge for an item. If you would like to set the price of an item during checkout (variable pricing), leave this blank.

Determine how you would like items priced: Fixed (the default price you set for an item), Variable (employees enter/choose a price for the item during checkout), or Per Unit (used for items that are charged by their weight). Note: if you select Per Unit, you will need to specify the unit of measurement in the "Price Unit" column.
Price unit is the unit of measurement for items that are charged by weight. Examples of price units are 'gram', 'ounce', 'pound', or an abbreviation such as 'lbs'.
The universal product code (UPC) for an item. On merchandise, this is the string of numbers and letters that appears below the barcode.
Stock count. Clover will automatically update stock count as items are purchased.
Revenue classes are specific categories of income. An example of a revenue class for a restaurant is "Alcohol," or "Protein."

Labels are used to route items from your POS to a specific receipt printer or Kitchen Display. Routing labels are useful for quick- and full-service restaurants and are necessary for using a Clover KDS.
Would you like this item to show in the Register app on your POS? Enter yes or no. Tip: Hide seasonal items from your Register app by entering no.
The stock keeping unit of an item. On merchandise, this is the string of numbers that appears above the barcode.
The number of an item currently in stock. Note: Clover will automatically update stock count as items are purchased.
To define a subcategory in the Categories sheet, put the name in the Subcategory Name column. Ensure that subcategories are listed directly under their parents.
Any applicable tax rates or fees for an item.
Flat fee that may be applied as tax.
"Yes" = default tax rate/ "No" = not set to default tax rate. If "No," you must set the type of tax rate.
A variant is a different version of the same item. An example of variants are t-shirt sizes. Variants are optional, but recommended for retail businesses.
A variant attribute is an umbrella term for variant options. For example, a t-shirt's attributes may be the size or color.
A variant option is the choice associated with the variant's attribute. For example, the attribute of size may have variant options such as small, medium, and large.
The wholesale cost for an item.
